sprint: Zero-Holder Scenes -- close the legacy fallback gap in _resolveBorder's border resolution
task: A follow-up found while chasing a user report that Creator still showed a Holder box for a Builder Scene the user says has zero Places. The earlier 'Object Strip Label + Zero-Holder Layout fixes' sprint's own stated intent was that _resolveBorder's Artwork Theme branch would skip resolving Frame/mat/wall chrome entirely when the active Layout's holders field is 0, but the implementation only ever gated that one branch -- the function still fell through to the older, artwork-independent Sprint 8.4.2 'Picture Holder defaults' (themeOptions.holder) fallback beneath it, which can resolve its own non-null border from a theme's generic cornerRadius/padding/shadow/fill defaults with no relationship to Places/Holders at all. Once that fallback's border came back non-null, render()'s artwork-placeholder-box code (gated only on the border being truthy, never on holder count) still drew the 'Tap to add your artwork' box for a Scene that genuinely has zero Places. Fixed by moving the zero-Holder check to the very top of _resolveBorder, before either branch runs, so zero-Holder now uniformly means no border resolves at all -- matching Builder's own Runtime Preview, which draws nothing for a Scene with no Places. Verified via a real Playwright A/B test (git stash-toggling the fix on a synthetic zero-Holder Scene, comparing full-panel-rect pixel diffs before/after the change): for a freshly-authored World Builder theme with no themeOptions.holder defaults set, the legacy fallback branch was already returning null even pre-fix, so this reproduction confirms the fix closes a real, disclosed gap without changing observable output for that particular case -- the user's own actual visible symptom is still being triaged separately (a second object in their real World, not yet root-caused), since it did not reproduce against this synthetic fixture. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite (including the earlier zero-Holder Object Strip assertion) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
